PostHog vs Plausible: Which Is Better?

Choosing between PostHog and Plausible? Here's a side-by-side comparison to help you decide.

Quick verdict: Choose PostHog if you need developers or product-teams. Choose Plausible if you prioritize indie-hackers or solo-devs.

PostHog

PricingFree self-hosted / Free cloud (1M events/mo) / $0.00025 per event after
Rating★★★★½ 4.5/5

Key Features

  • Event-based product analytics with autocapture
  • Session recording and replay for user behavior analysis
  • Feature flags with gradual rollout and A/B testing
  • Heatmaps and click maps for UI analysis
  • Funnels, trends, and retention analysis
  • Self-hostable open-source deployment option

Pros

  • Session replay is incredibly useful for understanding user behavior — you see exactly what users do, not just what they click
  • Feature flags with A/B testing are built in, so you don't need LaunchDarkly separately
  • Self-hosted option means zero data leaving your infrastructure; GDPR compliance is straightforward
  • Free tier (1M events/month on cloud) is generous enough for early-stage products
  • The product is built by developers for developers; the API and integration docs are excellent

Cons

  • Self-hosting requires significant infrastructure (PostgreSQL, Redis, ClickHouse, lots of RAM)
  • Session replay data can get expensive at scale; cloud pricing climbs quickly with event volume
  • Dashboards are powerful but have a learning curve; not as intuitive as Google Analytics for basic reporting
  • Some features (experiment results, correlation analysis) are still maturing
  • Smaller community than Google Analytics or Mixpanel means fewer shared templates and plugins

Plausible

Pricing$9/mo (10K pageviews) / $19/mo (100K)
Rating★★★★½ 4.6/5

Key Features

  • Privacy-first web analytics without cookies
  • Real-time visitor dashboard with key metrics
  • Lightweight tracking script (under 1KB)
  • Campaign URL tracking and goal conversion tracking
  • Email reports and API access
  • Self-hostable open-source option

Pros

  • Simplest analytics dashboard you'll ever use — unique visitors, pageviews, referrers, pages, countries. Done.
  • GDPR, CCPA, and PECR compliant out of the box; no cookie banner required
  • Lightweight script (under 1KB) that doesn't slow down your site
  • Self-hostable option for complete data ownership
  • Real-time analytics actually work and are useful, not just a novelty

Cons

  • Feature set is deliberately minimal — don't expect event tracking, funnels, or session recording
  • Pricing in tiers (10K, 100K, 1M pageviews) means unexpected traffic spikes can cost you
  • No mobile SDK for app analytics; it's purely for websites
  • Self-hosting requires a server and basic DevOps skills
  • The simplicity is a feature, but power users will miss Google Analytics' depth

Which Should You Choose?

Both PostHog and Plausible are excellent tools in their category. The right choice depends on your specific needs:

  • Choose PostHog if: PostHog is the open-source product analytics platform that's eating Mixpanel's lunch. It gives you event tracking, session recording, feature flags, A/B testing, and heatmaps in one self-hostable or cloud package. The killer feature is session replay — you can literally watch how users interact with your product. And because it's open-source, you own your data.
  • Choose Plausible if: Plausible is the lightweight, privacy-first Google Analytics alternative that gives you the metrics you actually need without the complexity. No cookie banners, no GDPR headaches, no dashboard full of vanity metrics you never look at. It's popular with indie hackers and small businesses who want to know how many people visit their site and where they come from — and nothing more.
We may earn a commission if you purchase through links. All opinions are our own.
